scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: All pass criteria met: 8 rules extracted with individual confidence/invariant ratings, 5+ clarifying questions surfaced as escalations, 'demand' explicitly identified as undefined (first escalation), no Given/When/Then acceptance criteria written (empty array), overall confidence.level is 30 (<=40), and no specific values were invented without flagging as assumptions.

observations.text: 8 well-reasoned business rules extracted, each with invariant classification and justification — exceeds the 5-rule minimum
5 escalations surfaced as formal clarifying questions covering demand definition, guardrail values, price-lock policy, emergency pricing, and data fallback behavior
Demand identified as critically undefined in the first escalation with concrete options for what it could mean
Acceptance criteria array is empty — correctly refused to write Given/When/Then from vague input
Confidence level of 30 with thorough reasoning listing high and low confidence areas separately
Assumptions are explicitly labeled as assumptions, not presented as requirements
Edge cases marked as UNSPECIFIED where the CEO gave no guidance — avoids inventing behavior
Invariant classifications are well-reasoned — guardrail existence (BR-4, BR-5) and positive price (BR-8) as invariants, pricing direction as policy
Testable assertions in business rules read like proto-acceptance-criteria (e.g., 'Given demand rises above...then...'), which is borderline Given/When/Then despite the empty acceptance_criteria array
Domain analysis and business impact sections are quite detailed for input this vague — slightly risks appearing as a complete analysis, though caveats are present throughout

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'ambiguity', got values: 'missing_context', 'missing_context', 'missing_context'
